messages.app.title: HA-MCP Settings
messages.language.label: Language
messages.theme.label: Theme
messages.notice.restart_required: ⚠ Changes saved. Restart HA-MCP for them to take effect. On next startup, HA-MCP fully removes disabled tools from the MCP tool list. Then reconnect or refresh the MCP server in your AI client (e.g. refresh tool list on claude.ai, re-add/refresh the connector in ChatGPT, or close and reopen Claude Desktop) so it reloads the tool list. Restarting the App (add-on) or Home Assistant does NOT refresh your client's cached tool list.
messages.notice.shared_settings: Server-wide features (Tool Search, YAML config editing, filesystem tools, etc.) appear in both the Server Settings tab and the App (add-on) Configuration page; they're the same settings either way. Either surface stays in sync with the other after the App (add-on) restarts. Changes require HA-MCP to be restarted.
messages.notice.env_pins: Tools listed in the DISABLED_TOOLS or PINNED_TOOLS env vars are locked read-only; unset the env var to edit them here. Pin toggles only take effect when Tool Search is enabled (Server Settings tab or App (add-on) Configuration page).
messages.tools.read_only.label: Read Only Mode
messages.tools.read_only.help: Turns off all write tools and blocks every tool from making write or destructive calls. Mixed read/write tools (backups, Apps (add-ons), energy preferences, voice pipelines, and code mode when enabled) stay available with their write operations blocked.
messages.tools.read_only.unknown: Could not read server settings. Read Only Mode status unknown; this view may not reflect what the server enforces.
messages.server.auto_save_notice: Settings on this tab save automatically. Changes take effect only after you restart the App (add-on) (applies them server-side) and reconnect or refresh the MCP server in your AI client (reloads the tool list), e.g. refresh tool list on claude.ai, re-add or refresh the connector in ChatGPT, or close and reopen Claude Desktop.
messages.server.sidecar.disable_title: Permanently disables the settings UI: stops this server AND writes ~/.ha-mcp/settings_ui_disabled so it does not respawn on future ha-mcp launches. Delete that file to re-enable.
messages.policies.title: Tool Security Policies
messages.policies.intro: Per-tool approval gating for high-stakes calls. Use the Tools tab to enable gating for a tool, then refine the matching conditions and approval lifetime here. Condition operators: equals, is one of, regex, contains, is present, greater than, less than.
messages.policies.global.title: Global settings
messages.policies.global.enabled.label: Enable Tool Security Policies
messages.policies.global.enabled.help: Master switch. Mirrors the toggle in Server Settings. Off by default. Toggle on and restart the App (add-on) to activate the gating middleware. While off, the rules below persist but aren't enforced.
messages.policies.global.wait.label: Wait seconds (5-600)
messages.policies.global.wait.help: How long the middleware waits for an approval before timing out.
messages.policies.global.ttl.label: Approval TTL minutes (1-60)
messages.policies.global.ttl.help: How long a pending approval stays in the queue before expiring.
messages.policies.pending.title: Pending approvals
messages.policies.rules.title: Gated tools
messages.policies.rules.empty: No tools currently security-gated. Enable per-tool gating from the Tools tab.
messages.accessibility.intro: These settings apply immediately and are saved in this browser and on the server, so they survive restarts in every mode (including stdio, where the settings page moves to a fresh port each session). The docs site offers the same controls in its navigation bar; it has no server and saves per browser.
messages.accessibility.storage_blocked: Your browser is blocking site storage; choices apply for this session only.
messages.accessibility.theme.title: Theme
messages.accessibility.theme.help: One-click color schemes. Auto, the default, follows your OS preference and flips live when it changes.
messages.accessibility.text_size.title: Text size
messages.accessibility.text_size.help: Scales the root font size. Browser zoom (Ctrl/Cmd +) still works on top of this.
messages.accessibility.colors.title: Custom colors
messages.accessibility.colors.help: Pick your own colors on top of the selected theme. Each swatch opens your system's visual color picker.
messages.accessibility.colors.low_contrast: The chosen background and text colors fall below a 4.5:1 contrast ratio and may be hard to read together.
messages.visibility.intro: Hide low-value entities (auto-generated diagnostic or helper entities, or an explicit list) from the collection read tools ha_search and ha_get_overview, so search and overview results are less cluttered. Off by default; changes apply on the next tool call.
messages.visibility.boundary: Noise reduction, not access control: a hidden entity is still returned by a direct read of its entity_id and still appears in automation, dashboard, and template content. Do not rely on this as a security boundary.
messages.visibility.categories.title: Hide by category
messages.visibility.categories.help: Home Assistant tags auto-generated entities as diagnostic or config.
messages.visibility.categories.hidden: Also entities already hidden in Home Assistant
messages.visibility.area_label.title: Hide by area or label
messages.visibility.area_label.help: Comma-separated area IDs or label IDs; entities in any listed area or label are hidden.
messages.visibility.entities.title: Hide specific entities
messages.visibility.entities.help: One entity_id per line. These stay hidden even without an entity-registry entry.
messages.visibility.allowlist.title: Restrict to an allowlist
messages.visibility.allowlist.help: Advanced: when any allowlist field is set the filter inverts – only entities matching an allowlist stay visible, everything else is hidden (including entities added later). Leave all three empty to disable.
messages.visibility.allowlist.entities_help: Allowed entity IDs, one per line.
messages.visibility.assist.title: Respect Assist exposure
messages.visibility.assist.help: Hide entities not exposed to Home Assistant's Assist (“conversation”) voice assistant, mirroring the Settings > Voice assistants > Expose list. Adds two websocket reads per search while enabled.
messages.visibility.assist.only_exposed: Only show entities exposed to Assist
messages.features.enable_tool_search.label: Enable tool search
messages.features.enable_tool_search.help: Replace the full tool catalog with search-based discovery. Reduces idle context from ~46K to ~5K tokens. ⚠️ Do NOT enable this if you use Claude in Sonnet or Opus modes — those models have their own built-in tool search / deferred tools, which conflicts with ours. To use ha-mcp's tool search with Claude, disable Claude's built-in tool search first; otherwise leave this off. Use this only with LLMs that lack native deferred tools (e.g. Claude Haiku, local OpenAI-compatible models) or with smaller context windows. Tools are found via ha_search_tools and executed via categorized proxies (read/write/delete). Requires restart to take effect.
messages.features.tool_search_max_results.label: Tool search max results
messages.features.tool_search_max_results.help: Maximum number of tools returned by ha_search_tools when tool search is enabled. Lower values (2-3) save context tokens but may miss relevant tools. Range: 2-10. Requires restart.
messages.features.enable_tool_security_policies.h: Gate high-stakes tool calls (lock/alarm control, automation writes, etc.) behind user approval. When a guarded tool is called, the agent tells the user to open the Tool Security Policies tab in the web UI and click Approve before the call proceeds. Per-tool rules with optional argument conditions are configured in the Tool Security Policies tab. Off by default. Requires restart to take effect.
messages.features.read_only_mode.label: Read Only Mode
messages.features.read_only_mode.help: Toggles all write tools off, and removes ability for tools to make any write or destructive calls. Mixed read/write tools (backups, add-ons, energy preferences, voice pipelines, and code mode when enabled) stay available with their write operations blocked. Same toggle as the web UI Tools tab. Off by default. Requires restart to take effect.
messages.features.enable_mandatory_bps.label: Attach best-practice skills on writes
messages.features.enable_mandatory_bps.help: Master switch for the write-tool skill content delivery feature (issue #1182). When enabled (default), the six config write tools (automations, scripts, scenes, helpers, dashboards, raw YAML) attach the canonical Home Assistant best-practice reference files under `skill_content` on every successful write, plus auto-embed any reference sections cited by best-practice warnings. Each tool also exposes a per-call `MandatoryBPS` parameter the agent can set to false on subsequent calls once it has the content. When this master switch is off, NO skill_content goes out regardless of the per-call parameter or BP warnings. Recommended ON as the first choice; disable only for models with very small context windows. Turning this off may degrade write accuracy. Requires restart to take effect.
messages.features.enable_strict_mandatory_bps.hel: Strict mode: prevents the client from using the tool until it can prove that it read the best practices. While on, the six best-practice write tools (automations, scripts, scenes, helpers, dashboards, raw YAML) are blocked and return an error directing the client to read the best-practices skill via ha_get_skill_guide and pass back the acknowledgment key it obtains there. While on, the ha_get_skill_guide tool is locked enabled — it is the only publisher of the acknowledgment key. Child of the "Attach best-practice skills on writes" option above and inert while that parent is off. Requires restart to take effect.
messages.features.enable_beta_features.label: Enable beta features (master)
messages.features.enable_beta_features.help: ⚠ DANGER — these tools can PERMANENTLY DAMAGE your Home Assistant installation. They write to your YAML config, your filesystem, install custom components, run arbitrary sandboxed Python, and edit tool docstrings the AI sees. There is no warranty and no support guarantee — you enable them at your OWN RISK. Take a Home Assistant backup before turning this on, and never enable in production without one. Master gate for the 5 experimental sub-flags below; sub-flags are ignored at runtime while this master is off, even when explicitly set to true. The same toggle is also surfaced in the web settings UI under "Beta features (dangerous)" — either surface reflects the other on restart.
messages.features.enable_yaml_config_editing.help: Beta feature. Allows AI assistants to add, replace, or remove top-level keys in configuration.yaml and packages/*.yaml. Only whitelisted keys are allowed (e.g., template, sensor, command_line, mqtt, knx); core keys like homeassistant, http, and recorder are blocked. Each edit validates YAML syntax, runs a config check, and creates an automatic backup. Changes to most keys require a full HA restart to take effect. See docs/beta.md for known limitations. Dedicated tools (automations, scripts, scenes, helpers, template sensors) should be preferred when available. REQUIRES the master "Enable beta features" toggle above (and in the web UI) to be on — otherwise this sub-flag is ignored at runtime regardless of its value here.
messages.features.enable_yaml_edit_confirm.label: Require confirmation for YAML edits (diff preview)
messages.features.enable_yaml_edit_confirm.help: Sub-toggle of YAML config editing, ON by default (recommended). The first ha_config_set_yaml call returns a unified diff of exactly what would change on disk plus a confirm token and writes nothing; the edit only lands when the call is repeated with that token. Exists so unintended changes outside the requested edit are caught before they reach disk (issue #1720). Turn off only to save one round-trip per edit.
messages.features.enable_yaml_packages_automation: Sub-toggle of YAML config editing. When on, ha_config_set_yaml accepts yaml_path='automation' inside packages/*.yaml. When off, the call is rejected both client-side and server-side. The storage-mode tool (ha_config_set_automation) is unaffected. Default OFF; only takes effect when "Enable YAML config editing" above is also on.
messages.features.enable_yaml_packages_script.hel: Sub-toggle of YAML config editing. When on, ha_config_set_yaml accepts yaml_path='script' inside packages/*.yaml. When off, the call is rejected both client-side and server-side. The storage-mode tool (ha_config_set_script) is unaffected. Default OFF; only takes effect when "Enable YAML config editing" above is also on.
messages.features.enable_yaml_packages_scene.help: Sub-toggle of YAML config editing. When on, ha_config_set_yaml accepts yaml_path='scene' inside packages/*.yaml. When off, the call is rejected both client-side and server-side. The storage-mode tool (ha_config_set_scene) is unaffected. Default OFF; only takes effect when "Enable YAML config editing" above is also on.
messages.features.enable_filesystem_tools.label: Enable filesystem tools (beta)
messages.features.enable_filesystem_tools.help: Sets HAMCP_ENABLE_FILESYSTEM_TOOLS=true. Enables direct file read/write access to your Home Assistant filesystem. WARNING: This gives the MCP server sensitive direct file access to your system. Only enable if you trust the AI assistant with file operations. Requires restart to take effect. REQUIRES the master "Enable beta features" toggle above (and in the web UI) to be on — otherwise this sub-flag is ignored at runtime regardless of its value here.
messages.features.enable_code_mode.label: Enable custom tool sandbox (beta)
messages.features.enable_code_mode.help: Beta feature. Enables the ha_manage_custom_tool tool, which lets AI assistants create, run, save, and delete custom Python code in a secure sandbox when no built-in tool can handle the request. Code runs in an isolated interpreter with no filesystem or arbitrary network access. Sandbox code can hit the HA REST API (api_get/api_post), send WebSocket commands (ws_send), call existing MCP tools (call_tool), or remove a saved tool (delete_saved_tool). Saved tools persist to /data/saved_tools.json by default so they survive add-on restarts, and are visible to any client that can connect. See docs/beta.md for known limitations. Requires restart to take effect. REQUIRES the master "Enable beta features" toggle above (and in the web UI) to be on — otherwise this sub-flag is ignored at runtime regardless of its value here.
messages.features.enable_lite_docstrings.label: Enable lite tool docstrings (beta)
messages.features.enable_lite_docstrings.help: Beta feature. Replaces the docstrings on a handful of heavy ha-mcp tools (automations, scripts, scenes, helpers, dashboards, ha_call_service, ha_config_set_yaml) with shorter variants that defer schema and example detail to the ha_get_skill_guide tool (or its skill:// resource). WARNING: this reduces idle token usage, but may degrade LLM performance — the trimmed descriptions rely on the LLM actually calling the skill tool or reading the skill resource for detail, which is not guaranteed (some models will skip the extra tool call and end up with less guidance than they had before). Best paired with a client that supports MCP resources or with enable_tool_search. Requires restart to take effect. REQUIRES the master "Enable beta features" toggle above (and in the web UI) to be on — otherwise this sub-flag is ignored at runtime regardless of its value here.
messages.features.enable_dashboard_screenshot.hel: Beta feature — disabled by default. Adds the ha_get_dashboard_screenshot tool plus include_screenshot / return_screenshot options on the dashboard get/set tools, so AI assistants can inspect one or more responsive Lovelace images (e.g. to verify a dashboard they just created). Supports stable named views, mobile/tablet/desktop batches, and PNG/JPEG/WebP/BMP output. Rendering runs in a separate, opt-in engine — balloob's "Puppet" add-on (headless Chromium) — which you install once (add balloob's add-on repository, then install "Puppet") and give a long-lived access token; on Docker/Container deployments you run that engine as a sidecar and set HAMCP_DASHBOARD_SCREENSHOT_ENGINE_URL. Nothing heavy is installed unless you both enable this and install the engine. Requires restart to take effect. REQUIRES the master "Enable beta features" toggle above (and in the web UI) to be on — otherwise this sub-flag is ignored at runtime regardless of its value here.
messages.actions.restart_confirm: Restart HA-MCP now? The page will reload automatically once it is back online.
messages.status.saved_llm_api: Saved. LLM API exposure applies on the next agent message.
messages.status.stopped_offline: Stopped. This page will go offline
messages.status.waiting_addon: Waiting for App (add-on) to come back online…
messages.errors.json_endpoint: Failed to parse {endpoint} response as JSON: {message}
messages.errors.render: Render failed: {message} (open browser devtools for the stack)
messages.errors.addon_not_back: App (add-on) did not come back online. Reload manually
messages.notice.restart.addon: ⚠ Changes saved. Click "Restart App (add-on)" for them to take effect. Disabled tools will be removed from the MCP tool list on next startup. Then refresh the tool list in your AI client; restarting the App alone does not refresh the client's cached list.
messages.notice.restart.embedded: ⚠ Changes saved. Click "Restart HA-MCP Server" to reload the in-process integration. Then refresh the MCP tool list in your AI client.
messages.notice.restart.sidecar: ⚠ Changes saved. Fully quit and reopen your MCP client for them to take effect. Disabled tools will be removed from the MCP tool list on next startup.
messages.notice.restart.standalone: ⚠ Changes saved. Restart the ha-mcp process, then refresh the MCP tool list in your AI client.
messages.server.sidecar.confirm_disable:
⚠ PERMANENTLY disable the settings server?

This stops the server and writes a marker that prevents it from starting again. To restore access, delete ~/.ha-mcp/settings_ui_disabled and unset HA_MCP_DISABLE_SETTINGS_UI. Continue?
messages.tools.empty: No tools found. The sidecar reads ~/.ha-mcp/tool_metadata.json. If it is missing or empty, restart your MCP client. See ~/.ha-mcp/sidecar.log for details.
messages.tools.group_toggle_title: Enable/disable all tools in this group
messages.tools.notes.beta_disabled: Beta. Set {setting} in the dev App (add-on) config or matching environment variable (see docs/beta.md).
messages.tools.notes.env_pinned: Pinned by {variable}. Unset the environment variable to edit here.
messages.tools.notes.bps_locked: Locked while Strict best-practices mode is on. Turn that setting off first to disable this tool.
messages.tools.notes.read_only_off: Off. Read Only Mode is on; write tools are disabled.
messages.tools.notes.read_only_partial: Read Only Mode: write operations of this tool are blocked; read operations stay available.
messages.tools.llm_api.label: LLM API
messages.tools.llm_api.help: Offer this tool to Home Assistant conversation agents through the LLM API. Applies on the next agent message; no restart is required.
messages.tools.aria.llm_api: {title} exposed to the conversation-agent LLM API
messages.tools.security.enable_first: Enable Tool Security Policies in App (add-on) config first.
messages.tools.read_only.save_failed: Read Only Mode change did not save. The server still has the previous value.
messages.backup.fields.enable_auto_backup.label: Auto-backup edits
messages.backup.fields.enable_auto_backup.help: Capture a snapshot before every wrapped write or destructive tool call.
messages.backup.fields.auto_backup_throttle_minut: Per-entity throttle. 0 = every write; N > 0 = at most one backup per N minutes. Range 0–1440.
messages.backup.fields.auto_backup_retain_per_ent: Maximum snapshots kept per entity (1–10000). Older ones rotate out.
messages.backup.fields.auto_backup_dir.label: Backup directory override
messages.backup.fields.auto_backup_dir.help: Leave empty to use the default directory; otherwise enter an absolute path.
messages.backup.fields.auto_backup_calendar_looka: How far ahead to query calendar events for pre-edit snapshots. Range 1–365.
messages.backup.fields.enable_snapshot_delete.hel: Allow ha_manage_backup to delete full HA snapshots, while keeping scheduled, newest, and too-young snapshots protected.
messages.backup.fields.snapshot_delete_min_age_da: A snapshot must be at least this old before deletion. Range 0–365.
messages.backup.origins.addon: Synced to Supervisor. Restart required after save.
messages.backup.origins.default: Using default; first save creates a local override file.
messages.backup.empty: No backups yet. Enable auto-backup and edit an entity to create one.
messages.backup.confirm.restore:
Restore {name}?

This overwrites the current entity state. A safety backup is taken first.
messages.backup.bulk.prompt_days: Delete backups older than N days (leave blank to use current filters only):
messages.backup.bulk.filter_required: Set at least one filter (Domain, Entity, or age in days).
messages.filesystem.custom.title: Custom filesystem directories (advanced)
messages.filesystem.custom.help: Extra directories (one per line) that file tools may read and write. Relative config paths and allowed HAOS sibling volumes are supported. Applies immediately.
messages.filesystem.custom.blocked: Always blocked: {paths}, path traversal (..), and absolute paths outside allowed HAOS volumes.
messages.filesystem.custom.enable_first: Enable beta features and filesystem tools above to edit.
messages.origins.env_locked: Set via environment variable; unset it to edit here.
messages.origins.addon_synced: Synced to the App (add-on) Configuration tab. Restart required after save.
messages.origins.env_var_unset: Set via environment variable {variable}; unset it to edit here.
messages.origins.beta_legacy_bridge: Auto-enabled in App (add-on) legacy mode. Set enable_beta_features explicitly in Configuration to take control. (env: {variable})
messages.origins.addon_runtime: Set by the App (add-on) runtime environment managed by Supervisor; cannot be changed here. (env: {variable})
messages.origins.display_only: Display only. Modify via environment variable or App (add-on) settings.
messages.features.errors.json: Feature flags response was not valid JSON.
messages.advanced.errors.json: Advanced settings response was not valid JSON.
messages.advanced.sidecar.stdio_only: Available in stdio mode only. This HTTP server has no settings-UI sidecar to pin.
messages.advanced.enable_dev_mode.confirm:
⚠ Enable developer mode?

After restart, hidden developer tools will be exposed to connected AI agents. Enable only for development and testing.
messages.advanced.code_mode_saved_tools_path.addo: Hardcoded to /data/saved_tools.json in App (add-on) mode so saved tools survive updates.
messages.advanced.code_mode_saved_tools_path.blan: If blank, custom tools are kept in memory only and lost on restart.
messages.policies.errors.conflict: {operation} failed: policy was modified in another tab or session. Reload and re-apply your changes.
messages.policies.errors.corrupt_suffix: (tool_policy.json appears corrupt; edit or delete it on the App (add-on) /data volume)
messages.policies.errors.update_tool: Failed to update tool security policy: {message}
messages.policies.errors.master_save: Tool Security Policies change did not save. The server still has the previous value.
messages.policies.card.no_conditions: (no conditions, rule matches every call to this tool)
messages.policies.card.conditions_intro: Require approval when ALL conditions match (no conditions = always require approval):
messages.policies.editor.any_argument_title: Match every call argument against this condition.
messages.policies.editor.value_source_http: value-source fetch failed (HTTP {status}); falling back to free-text
messages.policies.editor.value_source_error: value-source fetch failed ({message}); falling back to free-text
messages.policies.editor.schema_http: tool-schema fetch failed (HTTP {status}); falling back to free-text
messages.policies.editor.schema_error: tool-schema fetch failed ({message}); falling back to free-text
messages.policies.editor.hint.exists: Leave blank to gate on the argument being present regardless of value.
messages.policies.editor.hint.list: Pick one or more values, or type a JSON list. Leave blank to gate on any value.
messages.policies.editor.hint.regex: A regular expression to match the argument against.
messages.policies.editor.hint.contains: A substring or list item. Leave blank to gate on any value.
messages.policies.editor.hint.equals: The value the argument must equal. Leave blank to gate on any value.
messages.policies.pending.disabled: Tool Security Policies is off. Turn it on and restart the App (add-on) to enable gating.
messages.policies.pending.unavailable: Live approvals unavailable. Check the App (add-on) log.
messages.policies.pending.already_decided: This approval was already {decision}, possibly by another tab or session.
messages.policies.pending.invalid_token: This approval token is no longer valid (already consumed or expired).
messages.visibility.errors.corrupt_suffix: (entity_visibility.json appears corrupt; edit or delete it on the App (add-on) /data volume)
messages.visibility.errors.conflict: Config was changed in another tab or session. Reload the page, then re-apply your changes.
messages.advanced.homeassistant_url.label: Home Assistant URL
messages.advanced.homeassistant_url.help: Display only. Set via HOMEASSISTANT_URL or managed by the App (add-on).
messages.advanced.homeassistant_token.label: Home Assistant token
messages.advanced.homeassistant_token.help: Display only. Set via HOMEASSISTANT_TOKEN. Masked here for security.
messages.advanced.timeout.label: HA request timeout (s)
messages.advanced.timeout.help: Per-request HTTP timeout. Range 1–600. Restart required.
messages.advanced.max_retries.label: HA request max retries
messages.advanced.max_retries.help: Retry budget per failed REST call. Range 0–20. Restart required.
messages.advanced.verify_ssl.label: Verify SSL certificates
messages.advanced.verify_ssl.help: Disable TLS verification only on trusted networks. Restart required.
messages.advanced.fuzzy_threshold.label: Fuzzy-search threshold
messages.advanced.fuzzy_threshold.help: Lower values allow looser entity matching. Range 0–100.
messages.advanced.entity_search_limit.label: Entity search result limit
messages.advanced.entity_search_limit.help: Maximum entities returned by ha_search_entities. Range 1–1000.
messages.advanced.automation_config_time_budget.h: Maximum time deep search spends fetching automation configs. Range 1–600. Restart required.
messages.advanced.script_config_time_budget.label: Script config time budget (s)
messages.advanced.script_config_time_budget.help: Maximum time deep search spends fetching script configs. Range 1–600. Restart required.
messages.advanced.scene_config_time_budget.label: Scene config time budget (s)
messages.advanced.scene_config_time_budget.help: Maximum time deep search spends fetching scene configs. Range 1–600. Restart required.
messages.advanced.individual_config_timeout.label: Per-request config fetch timeout (s)
messages.advanced.individual_config_timeout.help: Timeout for each automation/script/scene config fetch. Range 1–600. Restart required.
messages.advanced.individual_fetch_batch_size.hel: Concurrent per-ID config fetches. Lower toward 1 on serial HA servers. Range 1–100. Restart required.
messages.advanced.backup_hint.label: Backup-hint level
messages.advanced.backup_hint.help: Controls how strongly the LLM is prompted to take a full HA snapshot before risky writes.
messages.advanced.dashboard_screenshot_engine_url: Base URL of the screenshot engine. Leave blank for Supervisor auto-discovery. Applies immediately.
messages.advanced.enable_websocket.label: Enable WebSocket
messages.advanced.enable_websocket.help: WebSocket state monitoring. Disabling falls back to polling and degrades many tools. Restart required.
messages.advanced.enabled_tool_modules.label: Enabled tool modules
messages.advanced.enabled_tool_modules.help: Comma-separated module names, or 'all'. Restart required.
Token-efficient partial dashboard tools. Disable for clients with programmatic tool use.
messages.advanced.mcp_server_name.label: MCP server name
messages.advanced.mcp_server_name.help: Reported in the MCP handshake. Restart required.
messages.advanced.mcp_server_version.label: MCP server version
messages.advanced.mcp_server_version.help: Defaults to the package version. Overriding may confuse clients. Restart required.
messages.advanced.environment.label: Environment
messages.advanced.environment.help: development or production. Affects logging verbosity. Restart required.
messages.advanced.log_level.label: Log level
messages.advanced.debug.label: Debug mode
messages.advanced.debug.help: Verbose request logging. May expose sensitive data. Restart required.
messages.advanced.code_mode_max_duration.label: Code-mode max duration (s)
messages.advanced.code_mode_max_duration.help: Wall-clock budget per sandbox run. Range 1–300. Restart required.
messages.advanced.code_mode_max_memory.label: Code-mode max memory (bytes)
messages.advanced.code_mode_max_memory.help: Memory cap per sandbox run. Range 1–256 MB. Restart required.
messages.advanced.code_mode_max_recursion.label: Code-mode max recursion
messages.advanced.code_mode_max_recursion.help: Recursion-depth cap per sandbox run. Restart required.
messages.advanced.code_mode_max_invocations.label: Code-mode max invocations
messages.advanced.code_mode_max_invocations.help: API/tool-call cap per sandbox run. Restart required.
messages.advanced.code_mode_saved_tools_path.help: JSON file used to persist custom tools across restarts. Restart required.
messages.advanced.extra_yaml_write_keys.label: Extra YAML write keys
messages.advanced.extra_yaml_write_keys.help: Comma-separated top-level keys ha_config_set_yaml may write in addition to the built-in ones, for YAML-first integrations on this install (e.g. alert2). Keys that redefine Home Assistant's own trust boundary can never be added and are ignored. Requires custom component 1.2.3 or newer.
messages.advanced.sidecar_pin_port.label: Settings UI sidecar port
messages.advanced.sidecar_pin_port.help: 0 chooses a free port each restart; 1024–65535 pins a preferred port. Restart required.
messages.advanced.enable_dev_mode.label: Developer mode
messages.advanced.enable_dev_mode.help: ⚠ Registers hidden tools that can change settings and replace the running server. Development and testing only. Restart required.
